questions.question: Colonial economic policies created a skewed distribution of income and wealth in Indian society. Which of the following is a key indicator of income inequality in colonial India?
What was the ultimate promise made by Bhagirath Majhi to the Santhals if they followed his teachings?
questions.option_4: Access to modern education and jobs

questions.question: Where was the headquarters of the Home Rule League founded by Bal Gangadhar Tilak located?
The partition of Bengal officially came into force in the year _______.

This freedom fighter proclaimed himself the Peshwa during the Revolt of 1857 and gathered armed forces and expelled the British garrison from the city. He was the adopted son of the late Peshwa Baji Rao. The leader in question is:
Which of the following names is associated with the Faraizi Movement?
The commercialisation of agriculture under the British transformed the rural economy. What did 'commercialisation of agriculture' in British India lead to?
Which law allowed inter-caste and inter-religious marriages during the British period in India?
Who was the Chief Architect behind the integration of over 560 princely states into the Indian Union?
Which tribal revolt in 1831-32 was led by Buddho Bhagat and targeted exploitative landlords and British officials?
The infamous Jallianwala Bagh massacre occurred when Gandhi called all the Indians to observe April 6, 1919, as a day of non-violent protest against which of the following colonial policies?
Which of the following leaders is associated with the concept of ‘Drain of Wealth’ theory?
When was the Mines Act enacted in India?
What was Gandhi’s view about the relation between means and ends in Satyagraha?
questions.option_1: Violence is necessary for pure ends.
questions.option_4: Only ends matter, not the means.

questions.question: The Kherwar movement was led by which charismatic leader?
Which Act ended the monopoly of the East India Company over trade with India?
During the 1857 Revolt, Indian sepoys declared which of the following as their symbolic leader?
The Non-cooperation Movement in 1920 included all the following, EXCEPT:
Which reformer is known for his efforts to promote widow remarriage and women’s education in Bengal?
On which of the following dates did Mahatma Gandhi begin a 21-day fast of self-purification to support the Harijan movement?
The first session of the Indian National Congress was presided by ______.
Which of the following could be regarded as the only positive outcome of the British reforms in India?
Hool Diwas is celebrated on June 30 every year in India to commemorate the Santhal Hool (rebellion) of _________.
In which of the following years was the town of Madrasapatnam, which later expanded and developed into modern-day Chennai, purchased by the East India Company (EIC) from local kings?
Who is regarded as the chief architect of India’s Green Revolution?
